safety_constraints: All loaded rows passed pricing_basis_state in {RANGE_ONLY, DRAFT_PRICING_ALLOWED, INTERNAL_DOGFOOD_ONLY}
All loaded rows have non-empty source_ref_id
All loaded rows have at least one of range_low_cents / range_high_cents / default_cost_cents
Materials schema-only sheets (MAT_*) were NOT loaded (schemas only, no data)
founder_review_required is preserved per-row; downstream must honor
PUBLIC_REFERENCE wage benchmarks are baseline only; never client-facing labor rate

trade_rows.item_name: Luxury vinyl plank (LVP) — material, standard grade
trade_rows.review_notes: HomeGuide $2-10/SF, Today's Homeowner $2-7/SF, Fixr $1.50-10/SF material-only. Premium LVP $6-10+/SF. San Diego mid-band.

trade_rows.item_name: LVP / LVT install labor — click-lock floating
trade_rows.review_notes: Today's Homeowner $1.50-3/SF; thebestlaminateflooring $1.50-3/SF click-lock. Glue-down runs higher.
Strong consensus: HomeGuide $4-16/SF, This Old House $4-16/SF, Fixr $3-16/SF (most pay $5-12). Wide because grade+method vary.
HomeGuide $11-25/SF, Angi $6-25/SF, bigbrohardwood $6-25/SF installed. Homewyse national avg $13.19-16.72/SF May 2026.
HomeGuide $9-20/SF, Angi $7-20/SF. Homewyse national avg $7.89-12.79/SF May 2026. Better for slab/condo than solid.
HomeGuide $3-8/SF, HomeAdvisor $3-6/SF, Angi engineered $3-10/SF. Nail-down low end, glue-down higher.
HomeGuide & HomeAdvisor both $2-8/SF. Common GGR remodel line when keeping existing floors elsewhere.

trade_rows.item_name: Old flooring removal / tear-out (carpet, tile, wood)
trade_rows.review_notes: Carpet $0.70-1.60/SF; tile/wood $2-4/SF; range $0.50-4 across sources. Cross-check with 01_Demolition DEMO-002.
Multiple LVP sources cite $1-3/SF for subfloor prep. Subfloor REPLACEMENT is separate ($2.20-4.75/SF) — see note.
WEAK-ish: carpet pad $0.75-1.75/SF sourced; carpet face material varies widely by grade. Break into grade-specific SKUs in review.
HomeAdvisor batt $0.30-1.50/SF material + install; HomeGuide attic batt $0.80-2.60/SF installed; tlsenergysavers $0.55-2.00/SF crawl. Cheapest option, performance-sensitive to fit.
HomeGuide blown-in $0.90-2.40/SF; tlsinsulation $0.60-2.50/SF; Angi $1.75-7.50/SF (existing walls higher). Attic floor is the common case.
tlsenergysavers open-cell $1.50-3.00/SF; sprayman $1.00-4.50/SF range incl. closed-cell. Requires licensed installer.
tlsenergysavers closed-cell $1.75-4.50/SF; HomeGuide attic spray $1.50-5.00/SF / $0.60-2.90 per board foot. Acts as vapor barrier.
Angi $1.50-3/SF; HomeGuide $1-2/SF; tlsenergysavers $1-2/SF. Common before re-insulating an older San Diego home.
HomeGuide $350-3000; Angi $300-1500. Lump-sum varies by ductwork/penetration count. Often bundled with insulation scope.
HomeAdvisor $300-1650; Angi/HomeGuide $300-2500; Modernize avg $1047; This Old House avg $477. Homewyse $632-967 May 2026. CA/coastal trends higher.
HomeAdvisor double-hung $400-1000; NerdWallet $150-650. Vinyl is the budget frame material. Confirm GGR's standard spec.
Angi $100-300/window (complex to $600); Modernize ~15% of total (~$149 avg). New-opening installs much higher.
Modernize $150-400/window glass-only. Cheaper than full unit. Tempered/impact glass adds $75-150+.
SlabWise $15-40/SF, dgfloors $20-60/SF, multiple $10-50/SF installed. Budget option; lower durability/resale.
SlabWise $40-175/SF, HonestCasa $55-175/SF (most $70-115), dgfloors $50-70/SF entry. Needs periodic sealing.
SlabWise $50-150/SF (51% market share 2026); truvine $50-185/SF; PGMSA $70-180+/SF. Most popular kitchen surface.
SlabWise $65-250/SF, truvine $75-250/SF. Soft, higher maintenance — better for low-traffic. Often a luxury-tier ALLOWANCE.
truvine $57-170/SF; SlabWise $70-150/SF. Harder/more heat-resistant than marble; growing popularity.
Cited $10-30/SF labor. Most sources bundle fabrication into installed $/SF — this row is for when labor is broken out.
truvine $8-13/SF removal + disposal. Include in bid to avoid surprise charges.
roofingcalculator $3.43-4.65/SF; Cobex $3.43-5/SF; Fixr $3-5/SF; Modernize $3.50-5/SF. Most affordable, 15-20yr life. Note: SF here = roof area incl. pitch+waste.
Fixr $4-8/SF; roofrivercity $4-8.50/SF; roofingcalculator $4.11-5.57/SF; costflowai materials $120-180/square. Industry standard, 25-30yr.

trade_rows.item_name: Luxury / designer asphalt shingle roof — installed
trade_rows.review_notes: Fixr $6.50-16/SF; Modernize $7-16/SF. Mimics slate/wood; 30yr+ life. Homewyse asphalt shingle install $5.09-6.66/SF May 2026 (mid-grade).

trade_rows.item_name: Asphalt shingles — material per square (100 SF)
trade_rows.review_notes: HomeGuide $80-280/square (3-tab) up to higher for premium; modernize $100-600/square. 1 square = 100 SF. A bundle covers 32-33 SF, $27-45.
HomeGuide $2-3/SF; Fixr $1.50-9.60/SF (wide); roofrivercity $2-3/SF. Labor is 60-70% of total. Steep pitch +25-50%.
HomeGuide $1-3/SF; modernize $1-4/SF. Multiple layers can double demo cost.
costflowai underlayment $0.50-1.50/SF; ice & water shield ~$1.50/SF. Ancillaries add 15-25% on top of shingle cost.

trade_rows.item_name: Central AC + gas furnace — full system replacement, installed
trade_rows.review_notes: hvacloadcalculate $7000-15000 typical; Modernize avg $11590-14100; Angi unit $3300-16000 + ~$1500 labor. Most common US system.

trade_rows.item_name: Air-source heat pump — full system, installed
trade_rows.review_notes: hvacloadcalculate central air-source HP $8000-15000; Angi ducted $4500-8000; Modernize $2799-7449 least expensive type. DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO HEAT PUMP GUYS (HPG) 2027 LAUNCH.
hvacloadcalculate $3500-10000 installed; Angi gas furnace unit $956-3870. 95% AFUE needed for tax credit.
budgetheating single-zone $2000-7000 (basic ~$3000); HomeGuide $2500-6000; Angi 12k BTU ~$3000. RELEVANT TO HPG.
HomeGuide multi-zone $6500-15000+; UniColorado $5088-25750+ whole-home; budgetheating up to $14500. Per-zone roughly $4000-8000.
Angi $1000-2700 per 1000 SF system; Modernize $2100-4000 for 2000 SF home add-on. Only if existing ductwork is poor or adding zones.

trade_rows.item_name: Add supplemental heat pump to existing furnace (dual-fuel)
trade_rows.review_notes: Angi $2500-6000 to add HP to existing furnace. Relevant HPG retrofit play.
HomeGuide $6-12/SF avg (basic from $4); Angi $4-8/SF; costflowai basic 4in $4-7/SF. Light-use, non-structural.
bhumicalculator 6in reinforced $8-14/SF; HomeGuide up to $18/SF for thick/complex. Required for vehicle traffic / structural.
HomeGuide slab foundation $6-14/SF ($12-28k for 2000 SF home); remoteestimation $6-14/SF. Common in warm CA climate.
Angi stem wall $7000-21000 typical; more excavation/materials than monolithic slab. Per-SF inferred from total — verify.
Angi pier & beam $6-12/SF ($8000-15000 avg). Good for moisture/flood-prone; elevates structure.
HomeGuide $3-5/SF labor incl. form creation; costflowai $3-5/SF (finishers in short supply, pushing rates up).
costflowai $125-165/cubic yard; HomeGuide ~$110/CY. UOM = cubic yard. Tariff volatility noted in import-dependent coastal markets.
costflowai rebar grid $0.50-1.00/SF; remoteestimation $2-3/SF; wire mesh $0.15-0.30/SF. Required for vehicle traffic / by code.
HomeGuide stamped $8-19/SF incl. slab; Angi $8-20/SF. Stained/polished $3-15/SF (not incl. slab).

trade_rows.item_name: Grading / leveling — yard or building pad
trade_rows.review_notes: HomeGuide grading $0.40-2.00/SF; remoteestimation $0.40-2/SF; Angi foundation grading $2-10/SF (steeper). Soil + slope drive it.
costflowai excavation + grading $500-2500 depending on existing conditions. Highly site-specific.
costflowai compacted gravel base (4-6in) $1-2/SF. Standard prep layer under slabs.
costflowai 6-mil poly vapor barrier $0.10-0.20/SF. Standard under slab-on-grade.
bhumicalculator $6-12/SF; This Old House $6.36-9.66/SF; Homewyse $10.69-17.33/SF Jan 2026. Most common in San Diego/SW. 4-6% annual increase noted.
This Old House synthetic $10-18/SF; wallderstucco $9.30-16.90/SF. EIFS adds insulation; premium pricing.
Angi stucco labor $2-5/SF or $50-80/hr. Labor-intensive, experience-dependent.
HomeGuide $8-50/SF; Angi $8-50/SF ($600-2647 typical). Flat surfaces low end; around windows/doors high end. Common GGR exterior line.
This Old House vinyl siding $2.15-5.25/SF. Alternative exterior; less common in San Diego stucco market.
This Old House exterior painting $1-4/SF. Resolves the v0.1 PANT-007 BLOCKED row — but still confirm against GGR exterior jobs.

trade_rows.item_name: Basement / below-grade waterproofing (premium adder over slab)
trade_rows.review_notes: WEAK: estimators.us notes foundation cost 'affects excavation, waterproofing' and basement foundations run $5-30/SF total — but NO clean standalone waterproofing $/SF was found. Range left at 0/0 deliberately; treat as effectively BLOCKED until a direct source or GGR actual. San Diego has few basements anyway.
HomeGuide $25-50/SF; Ergeon $25-35/SF; bhumicalculator $20-30/SF installed. Cheapest deck; needs sealing every 2-3yr. Note: lumber volatile in 2026 (tariff pressure).
HomeGuide composite $40-80/SF, Trex $45-70/SF; Ergeon mid-grade $35-60/SF; bhumicalculator Trex $45-55/SF installed. 40-60% more upfront than PT, lower lifecycle cost.
HomeGuide cedar $30-47/SF, redwood $28-54/SF; Ergeon cedar $30-45/SF. Natural rot resistance; needs annual resealing.
HomeGuide Ipe $50-70/SF; Ergeon tropical hardwood $55-80/SF. 40-70yr lifespan; dense, slow to install.
Angi composite $12-22/SF material; decks.com $5-14/SF; DecksDirect ~$13/SF. Wide by tier (capped vs uncapped, brand).
Angi PT $2-5/SF material; decks.com $3-6/SF; DecksDirect ~$5/SF. Decking boards only — framing lumber separate.
HomeGuide $11-22/SF (PT, 1-story); Trex Protect $15-35/SF; decks.com $8-22/SF. Labor is 50-70% of total. Elevated/multi-level much higher.
HomeGuide wood deck railing $20-50/LF. Code-required on elevated decks. Stainless upgrade $50-250/LF.
HomeGuide deck stairs $500-2000 incl. concrete landing pad. Highly dependent on rise/run count.
HomeGuide stain + seal $1.50-4.50/SF labor + materials. Recurring maintenance line for wood decks.

trade_rows.item_name: Outdoor kitchen — built per square foot (full custom)
trade_rows.review_notes: Fixr avg $75/SF (range $40-130); Angi $200-600/SF (higher — different scope basis); JML $33-130/SF. WIDE because 'outdoor kitchen' scope varies enormously. Treat as rough planning anchor only.

trade_rows.item_name: Outdoor kitchen — built per linear foot (island basis)
trade_rows.review_notes: RTA Outdoor Living $1200-2000/LF built-from-scratch incl. base, finish, countertop, grill head, appliances. Manufacturer source — verify against GGR/sub bids.
HomeGuide built-in BBQ island $3000-10000 installed (grills up to 3ft, basic hookups); Fixr cabinets+counters around rolling grill ~$7500 avg. Prefab island unit alone $1500-6000.
HomeGuide pre-built modular $7000-16000 installed incl. shipping, assembly, hookups (existing utilities). Appliances pre-installed.
HomeGuide outdoor kitchen framework $200-700/LF (wood/aluminum cheapest); cabinets $900-4000 materials total. Weather-resistant materials. Valle could potentially supply these — flag tenant-vs-seed.
RTA Outdoor Living $350-1000/LF (recommends $600+/LF for weather durability). Cross-ref v0.3 12_Countertops for indoor basis.
LawnLove $0.90-1.80/SF; LawnGuru $2-5/SF; Landscape Associates CA $3-7/SF (prep+sod). San Diego likely mid-upper. Instant lawn vs seeding.
LawnLove grass seeding $0.10-0.19/SF. Cheaper than sod but slow to establish.
HomeGuide $10-25/SF; LawnLove $5.50-19.75/SF; Landscape Associates CA $12-28/SF. RELEVANT to drought-conscious San Diego (water rebates available). 15-25yr life.
HomeGuide $35-65/SF; Landscape Associates CA $60-120/sq face foot. Walls over 4ft typically need permit + structural engineering. Slope/soil drive it.
LawnGuru hardscape $15-50/SF; LawnGuru patios/walkways $20-35/SF; Fixr bluestone pavers ~$30/SF. Standard concrete pavers low end, natural stone high.
HomeGuide softscaping $9-12/SF; LawnGuru $4-10/SF. NOTE: large specimen trees ($500-2500+ each) move totals far beyond per-SF — itemize plants separately.
HomeGuide sprinkler system $3000-10000 (lawn-size dependent); drip irrigation $300-1200/zone; constructlytools $2500-5000 full yard. Lump-sum varies by yard size.
LawnLove land grading $650-3600; HomeGuide grading by SF $0.40-2.00. Cross-ref v0.3 16_Site_Prep SITE-001.
constructlytools mulch beds $1-3/SF installed. Low-cost groundcover. Common front-yard refresh line.
HomeGuide French drain $10-35/LF. Cross-ref v0.3 18_Waterproofing WPRF-003. Depth/soil-dependent.
LawnLove tree removal $385-1070; new tree planting $25-3000. Size-dependent. Common site-prep line.
HomeGuide fencing $5-25/LF by material (vinyl/wood lower, metal higher); LawnLove $2000-4500 typical project. Height-dependent — check permit limits.

labor_benchmarks.bls_occupation_name: Heating, Air Conditioning, and Refrigeration Mechanics and Installers
Drywall Installers, Ceiling Tile Installers, and Tapers
